comment_text.sn22.4:1.4: Once again Hāliddikāni names and quotes an abstruse passage (see DN 21:2.6.12 and note).
comment_text.sn22.4:3.1: The set of defilements here express a gradual process: from being attracted to something, to picking it up, solidifying a sense of ownership in one’s mind, then insisting on clinging to it, until finally it becomes an inherent part of you. See SN 12.15:2.4 and note.

root_text.sn22.4:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā mahākaccāno avantīsu viharati kuraraghare papāte pabbate.
root_text.sn22.4:1.3: Atha kho hāliddikāni gahapati yenāyasmā mahākaccāno …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho hāliddikāni gahapati āyasmantaṁ mahākaccānaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn22.4:1.5: ‘ye te samaṇabrāhmaṇā taṇhāsaṅkhayavimuttā, te accantaniṭṭhā accantayogakkhemino accantabrahmacārino accantapariyosānā seṭṭhā devamanussānan’ti.
root_text.sn22.4:2.1: Imassa nu kho, bhante, bhagavatā saṅkhittena bhāsitassa kathaṁ vitthārena attho daṭṭhabbo”ti?
root_text.sn22.4:3.1: “Rūpadhātuyā kho, gahapati, yo chando yo rāgo yā nandī yā taṇhā ye upayupādānā cetaso adhiṭṭhānābhinivesānusayā, tesaṁ khayā virāgā nirodhā cāgā paṭinissaggā ‘cittaṁ suvimuttanti’ vuccati.
root_text.sn22.4:4.4: viññāṇadhātuyā kho, gahapati, yo chando yo rāgo yā nandī yā taṇhā ye upayupādānā cetaso adhiṭṭhānābhinivesānusayā, tesaṁ khayā virāgā nirodhā cāgā paṭinissaggā ‘cittaṁ suvimuttanti’ vuccati.
root_text.sn22.4:5.1: Iti kho, gahapati, yaṁ taṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā sakkapañhe:
root_text.sn22.4:5.2: ‘ye te samaṇabrāhmaṇā taṇhāsaṅkhayavimuttā te accantaniṭṭhā accantayogakkhemino accantabrahmacārino accantapariyosānā seṭṭhā devamanussānan’ti.
root_text.sn22.4:6.1: Imassa kho, gahapati, bhagavatā saṅkhittena bhāsitassa evaṁ vitthārena attho daṭṭhabbo”ti.

translation_text.sn22.4:1.2: At one time Venerable Mahākaccāna was staying in the land of the Avantis near Kuraraghara on Steep Mountain.
translation_text.sn22.4:1.3: Then the householder Hāliddikāni went up to Venerable Mahākaccāna … and asked him,
translation_text.sn22.4:1.4: “Sir, this was said by the Buddha in ‘The Questions of Sakka’:
translation_text.sn22.4:1.5: ‘Those ascetics and brahmins who are freed due to the ending of craving have reached the ultimate goal, the ultimate sanctuary from the yoke, the ultimate spiritual life, the ultimate end, and are best among gods and humans.’
translation_text.sn22.4:2.1: How should we see the detailed meaning of the Buddha’s brief statement?”
translation_text.sn22.4:3.1: “Householder, consider any desire, greed, relishing, and craving for the form element; any attraction, grasping, mental fixation, insistence, and underlying tendencies. With the ending, fading away, cessation, giving away, and letting go of that, the mind is said to be ‘well freed’.
translation_text.sn22.4:4.1: Consider any desire, greed, relishing, and craving for the feeling element …
translation_text.sn22.4:4.4: the consciousness element; any attraction, grasping, mental fixation, insistence, and underlying tendencies. With the ending, fading away, cessation, giving away, and letting go of that, the mind is said to be ‘well freed’.
translation_text.sn22.4:5.1: So, householder, that’s how to understand the detailed meaning of what the Buddha said in brief in ‘The Questions of Sakka’:
translation_text.sn22.4:5.2: ‘Those ascetics and brahmins who are freed due to the ending of craving have reached the ultimate goal, the ultimate sanctuary from the yoke, the ultimate spiritual life, the ultimate end, and are best among gods and humans.’”
